statement: A uniform solid sphere of mass 2 kg and radius 0.15 m rolls without slipping down a 30° incline. Find the linear acceleration of the center of the sphere. For a solid sphere, I_G = (2/5)·m·r². Use g = 9.81 m/s².
common_student_errors: Using I_G = ½·m·r² (disk formula) instead of I_G = (2/5)·m·r² for a solid sphere
Forgetting the rolling constraint a = α·r to relate linear and angular accelerations
Getting the effective mass ratio wrong: for rolling, effective inertia = m(1 + I/(mr²)) = m·(1 + 2/5) = (7/5)m for a sphere
